title: Create requirements asset directory structure
notes: The packet is preserved in a stable versioned import tree, but the repo has not adopted the work packet's recommended docs/standards/hla-1516-2010 layout as the canonical location.

title: Place v1.0 catalog assets as canonical sources
notes: The canonical packet assets are imported and versioned, but the repo still treats the top-level curated requirements files as the working canon rather than flattening the packet's latest/ files into a repo-native canonical path.

title: Define requirements CSV schema contract
notes: The imported packet schema is preserved and repo-native verification tests now validate required columns, bounded core categories, canonical row counts, and the packet's two currently known parent-reference gaps, but there is not yet a reusable repo-owned schema module for the full canonical v1.0 CSV contract.

title: Add requirements loader module
notes: The repo has requirement-loading and aggregation code for curated ledgers and generated compliance artifacts, plus packet-manifest readers in verification tests, but not a small standalone typed loader centered on the packet's canonical CSV bundle.

title: Add requirements lint command
notes: Verification-oriented tests and CI lint stages now include packet manifest validation, required-column checks, verification-matrix foreign-key checks, and clause-tracker coverage checks, but there is not yet a dedicated requirements lint command that centralizes those checks behind one repo-supported interface.

title: Hook pytest tests to requirement IDs
notes: Requirement-to-test linkage is captured in curated CSVs and generated matrices, but tests do not yet carry direct pytest requirement markers or metadata emitted from the test layer itself.

title: Add CI validation for requirements assets
notes: CI already runs repo lint and verification tests, and those tests now include imported packet manifest integrity plus canonical CSV schema and referential-integrity checks, but there is not yet a dedicated canonical-requirements asset gate with its own explicit CI stage.

title: Generate human-readable requirements docs
notes: The repo generates and ships multiple human-readable requirements and compliance views, but not yet the exact packet-style markdown set grouped directly from the canonical v1.0 master catalog by standard, clause, capability, and service group.

title: Map requirements to implementation modules
notes: The harmonized clause bridges now map packet-derived requirements to concrete implementation areas and repo-native tests across Clauses 5 through 11.

title: Peer-review source clause traceability
notes: The repo still treats the imported packet as an engineering baseline rather than a certified clause-by-clause legal trace, and no separate reviewed-status pass against the restricted standards inputs has been completed.

title: Create implementation work queues from requirements
notes: The clause reconciliation files now function as executable implementation backlogs inside the repo, but they have not yet been turned into formal issue/project queues outside the CSV-ledger workflow.

title: Decide handling of restricted standards inputs
notes: The repo explicitly preserves generated catalogs while excluding restricted IEEE source PDFs and zip artifacts from the committed import tree.
